questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: You are program director in ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation) to conduct a feasibility study into the SDI (Strategic Defence Initiative) missile defence system. You are to consider the viability of a ground based laser defence against ICBM's, which will use powerful $\mathrm{CO}_2$ lasers to bore holes in the incoming missiles, thus destroying them. The lasers have an output power of 50 W in a beam diameter of 1 mm . The laser beam is fired at the missile when it is 10 km away and the beam loses $3 \%$ of its intensity for every kilometre travelled. The outer skin of the missile is aluminium that is 3 cm thick. When the laser fires the skin temperatures of the missile is $-50^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and must be heated to its boiling point at $2500^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. The density of aluminium is $2.4 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{cm}^3$ and heat capacity is $0.9 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{g}^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$.Question: The amount of heat required to reach the outer skin of aluminium to its boiling point is
questions.solution:
Amount of heat required to vaporise
$=\operatorname{ms} \Delta \theta=\left[\pi\left(0.5 \times 10^{-3}\right)^2 \times 3 \times 10^{-2} \times 2.4 \times 10^3\right]\left[0.9 \times 10^3\right][2550]=129.7 \mathrm{~J}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

Paragraph: By varying the voltage applied to the kettle, you can change power consumption P. Depending on the P kettle with water can be heated to different maximum temperatures. This dependence is shown in Table-1 :
Table-2 shows the variation of the temperature with time when the kettle of power 300 W is switched off. The heat capacity of the empty kettle $\mathrm{C}_0=100 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{K}$, specific heat of water $\mathrm{c}=4200 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{kg} \mathrm{K}$, the density of water $\rho=1000 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{m}^3$. Table-2 :
Question: The rate of heat loss at $t=600 \mathrm{sec}$ after the heater is switched off (as in table-2) is:

You are to consider the viability of a ground based laser defence against ICBM's, which will use powerful $\mathrm{CO}_2$ lasers to bore holes in the incoming missiles, thus destroying them. The lasers have an output power of 50 W in a beam diameter of 1 mm . The laser beam is fired at the missile when it is 10 km away and the beam loses $3 \%$ of its intensity for every kilometre travelled. The outer skin of the missile is aluminium that is 3 cm thick. When the laser fires the skin temperatures of the missile is $-50^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and must be heated to its boiling point at $2500^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. The density of aluminium is $2.4 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{cm}^3$ and heat capacity is $0.9 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{g}^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$.Question: The power of laser beam when it strikes missile surface is (Take $\left.(0.97)^{10}=0.738\right)$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: By varying the voltage applied to the kettle, you can change power consumption P. Depending on the P kettle with water can be heated to different maximum temperatures. This dependence is shown in Table-1 :
Table-2 shows the variation of the temperature with time when the kettle of power 300 W is switched off. The heat capacity of the empty kettle $\mathrm{C}_0=100 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{K}$, specific heat of water $\mathrm{c}=4200 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{kg} \mathrm{K}$, the density of water $\rho=1000 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{m}^3$. Table-2 :
Question: If the power consumption is 400 W
options: The water will come to the boiling point and start boiling
The water will reach boiling point but will not boil
The water will not reach boiling point but start boiling
The water will neither reach boiling point nor start boiling.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{\mathrm{dQ}}{\mathrm{dt}}=\mathrm{k}\left(\mathrm{~T}-\mathrm{T}_0\right) \\
& 100=\mathrm{k}(40-20) \\
& \mathrm{k}=5 \mathrm{~W} /{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C} \\
& 400=5(\mathrm{~T}-20) \\
& \mathrm{T}=100^{\circ} \mathrm{C}
\end{aligned}$
So water comes to Bioling point but entire energy is radiated. So it won't boil.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
